commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Ephraim. That is, fruitful, or growing. (Challoner)
Being in the plural number, it means "productions. "
Poverty; where I have been poor and afflicted, though now advanced in honour. (Haydock)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Notice that this child’s name too is suggestive of gratitude. “Not only did he grant me forgetfulness of my distress,” he is saying, “but he also made me prosper in the land where I suffered such awful humiliation as to be reduced to the limit and run a risk to life itself.”

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
